data.title: Zhejiang Expressway Forms Connected Joint Venture for Low-Altitude Infrastructure
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest announcement is out from Zhejiang Expressway Co ( (HK:0576) ).

Zhejiang Expressway Co has signed a cooperation agreement with Technology Research Institute, ZJIC, Zhejiang Information and Commercial Group to form a new joint venture focused on low-altitude infrastructure. The venture will invest in, build and operate facilities supporting low-altitude applications, signalling the company’s move to diversify beyond traditional expressways into emerging infrastructure segments.

Because all partners are subsidiaries of the company’s controlling shareholder, Communications Group, the deal is classified as a connected transaction under Hong Kong listing rules. With applicable percentage ratios above 0.1% but below 5%, the transaction will require reporting and public announcement, but is exempt from independent shareholder approval, streamlining execution while maintaining disclosure obligations.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:0576) stock is a Buy with a HK$7.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Zhejiang Expressway Co stock, see the HK:0576 Stock Forecast page.

More about Zhejiang Expressway Co

Zhejiang Expressway Co is a Chinese joint stock company listed in Hong Kong that primarily operates in the transportation infrastructure sector. The company’s core business centres on the investment, construction, and operation of expressways and related infrastructure in Zhejiang province, positioning it as a key player in regional transport and logistics development.

data.description:

Wisdom Sports Group ( (HK:1661) ) has shared an announcement.

Wisdom Sports Group has agreed, via a wholly owned subsidiary, to acquire a 49% equity stake in an unnamed target company from an independent vendor for RMB19.6 million, or about HK$22.1 million. Following completion, the target will remain a non-subsidiary associate and its financial results will not be consolidated into Wisdom Sports Group’s accounts.

The deal qualifies as a discloseable transaction under Hong Kong listing rules, triggering reporting and announcement requirements but not shareholder approval. Completion is contingent on due diligence and regulatory consents, and the company has cautioned investors that the transaction may not proceed if conditions are not satisfied or waived, underscoring execution risk around this strategic investment.

More about Wisdom Sports Group

Wisdom Sports Group, listed in Hong Kong, operates in the sports-related business sector. The company engages through subsidiaries in investments and transactions that expand its portfolio of interests in sports and related commercial ventures within mainland China and the broader regional market.

data.description:

  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Seacon Shipping Group Holdings Ltd. ( (HK:2409) ) has provided an update.

Seacon Shipping Group Holdings Ltd. has agreed to sell two 48,785-gross-ton bulk carriers, Seacon Vancouver and Seacon Oslo, both built in 2023, to an independent buyer for a total consideration of USD72.7 million. The vessels are currently chartered to Seacon’s subsidiaries under bareboat charters, and the company intends to first acquire them by exercising existing purchase options before delivering them to the buyer.

The disposal and the exercise of each purchase option each qualify as discloseable transactions under Hong Kong Listing Rules, with the relevant percentage ratios exceeding 5% but below 25%. The deals underscore Seacon’s active management of its fleet portfolio and capital structure, and they trigger reporting and announcement obligations without requiring shareholder approval, signaling a moderate but notable rebalancing of its vessel assets for investors to monitor.

More about Seacon Shipping Group Holdings Ltd.

Seacon Shipping Group Holdings Ltd. is a Cayman Islands-incorporated company listed in Hong Kong that operates in the maritime shipping industry, focusing on owning and chartering bulk carrier vessels. Its fleet includes modern bulk carriers that are typically deployed under bareboat and other chartering arrangements to serve global dry bulk cargo trade.

  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

NSING Technologies Inc. Class H ( (HK:2701) ) just unveiled an update.

Nsing Technologies Inc. reported unaudited first-quarter 2026 results showing total operating income of RMB 394.8 million, up from RMB 304.0 million a year earlier, indicating solid top-line growth. However, total operating costs rose to RMB 420.7 million from RMB 337.2 million, driven by higher operating, sales, R&D and finance expenses, leaving the group with a loss at the operating level despite gains in fair value income and other gains.

The company’s significant R&D spending of RMB 43.6 million and elevated finance costs of RMB 17.9 million underscore its continued investment-led strategy and leveraged capital structure. While fair value gains and other income partially offset cost pressures, the overall cost base is expanding faster than revenue, which may concern investors focused on profitability and capital efficiency in the near term.

More about NSING Technologies Inc. Class H

Nsing Technologies Inc. is a joint stock company incorporated in the People’s Republic of China and listed on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange. The group operates in the technology sector, deriving its revenue from operating income in renminbi, and invests in research and development as a key component of its cost structure.

data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions

The latest update is out from NSING Technologies Inc. Class H ( (HK:2701) ).

Nsing Technologies Inc. plans to replace its current domestic auditor, China Audit Asia Pacific Certified Public Accountants LLP, with Deloitte Touche Tohmatsu Certified Public Accountants LLP for the 2026 financial year, while reappointing Deloitte Touche Tohmatsu in Hong Kong as its overseas auditor. The move, which is subject to shareholder approval at the 2025 annual general meeting, is intended to align domestic and international audit work within the same network to enhance efficiency, consistency of audit methodologies, and handling of complex cross-border reporting issues.

The company cited the growing scale and complexity of its operations as the rationale for consolidating audit responsibilities within the Deloitte network, expecting better coordination through shared platforms, resources, and communication channels. Management will be authorised to negotiate audit fees for 2026 with a proposed cap of RMB 2.5 million, and both the company and the outgoing auditor reported no disagreements, signalling a smooth transition for investors and regulators.

More about NSING Technologies Inc. Class H

Nsing Technologies Inc. is a joint stock company incorporated in the People’s Republic of China and listed in Hong Kong, with its shares also linked to the ChiNext market in Shenzhen. The group operates businesses of sufficient scale and cross-border complexity to require coordinated domestic and international audits under both PRC accounting standards and international financial reporting standards.

data.title: RUSAL Pays RUB 426.3 Million Coupon on BO-001P-09 Exchange-Traded Bonds
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

United Co RUSAL ( (HK:0486) ) has issued an update.

United Company RUSAL has disclosed that it paid coupon income on its BO-001P-09 series exchange-traded bonds for the twenty-second coupon period running from 24 March 2026 to 23 April 2026. The total payout amounted to RUB 426.3 million, with bondholders receiving RUB 14.21 per bond across 30 million bonds.

The coupon on these exchange-traded bonds is calculated daily over the circulation period, using the Bank of Russia key rate as of the seventh day before the calculation date plus an additional 2.20% per annum S-spread. The announcement underscores RUSAL’s continued servicing of its ruble-denominated debt instruments on the Moscow Exchange, signalling ongoing access to and activity in the domestic bond market.

More about United Co RUSAL

United Company RUSAL is an international public joint-stock company and a major producer in the global aluminium industry. Listed in Hong Kong and on Russian exchanges, the group focuses on producing aluminium and related products for industrial and manufacturing customers worldwide.

An announcement from New Gonow Recreational Vehicles, Inc. ( (HK:0805) ) is now available.

New Gonow Recreational Vehicles reported audited annual results for 2025 showing revenue of RMB871.4 million, largely flat year-on-year, while gross profit fell 11.7% to RMB237.2 million and gross margin narrowed from 31.1% to 27.2%. Management attributed the margin pressure to macroeconomic volatility, an unexpected downturn in the RV market, and the lower initial margins from its new hybrid model, even as cost of sales rose 6.5% on higher volumes.

The company indicated that trading in its shares will resume following the publication of these results, marking a step toward normalizing its status on the Hong Kong market. The combination of resilient top-line performance but weaker profitability underscores both the challenges in the current RV cycle and the near-term cost of pursuing market expansion with new product lines and dealership arrangements in key regions such as Australasia.

More about New Gonow Recreational Vehicles, Inc.

New Gonow Recreational Vehicles Inc., incorporated in the Cayman Islands and listed in Hong Kong, operates in the recreational vehicle industry, focusing on RV products including a new hybrid model and electric RVs for markets such as China and Australasia. The group distributes through joint-venture dealers and stores, and leverages partnerships like its Green RV dealership agreement in Australia to expand its international footprint.

Chongqing Rural Commercial Bank Co ( (HK:3618) ) just unveiled an update.

Chongqing Rural Commercial Bank has secured regulatory approval from the Chongqing office of the NFRA for amendments to its Articles of Association, which took effect upon approval and will be published on both the Hong Kong exchange disclosure platform and the bank’s own website. The move reflects ongoing alignment of the bank’s governance framework with regulatory guidance and its specific operating circumstances.

Following the effectiveness of the amended Articles of Association, the bank has formally cancelled its Board of Supervisors, transferring its statutory oversight functions and powers to the audit committee of the Board and abolishing related governance rules. All members of the fifth Board of Supervisors have stepped down from their roles without disagreement with management, signalling a planned restructuring of oversight rather than a dispute-driven change and marking a shift toward a board committee–centric governance model.

data.title: WeRide Raises 2025 Cap on Connected Mapping and Data Services Deal
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest update is out from WeRide Inc. Class A ( (HK:0800) ).

WeRide Inc. has revised the 2025 annual cap for its continuing connected transactions under a mapping and data services procurement framework with related party Guangzhou Yuji, raising the limit from RMB65 million to RMB69 million after management determined the original cap would be insufficient. The Audit Committee approved the revision in November 2025, later ratified by the Board, with actual 2025 spending reaching RMB66.8 million, triggering reporting, announcement, and annual review requirements under Hong Kong listing rules but exempting the company from circular and independent shareholder approval obligations.

The framework agreement, effective from the company’s listing date through December 31, 2027, covers high-definition map production and data services essential to WeRide’s autonomous driving operations. The adjustment of the 2025 cap, set alongside existing caps of RMB70 million for 2026 and 2027, underscores continued demand for mapping and data services while highlighting ongoing governance and compliance oversight for connected-party arrangements in its supply chain.

WeRide Inc. is a Cayman Islands-incorporated company operating in the autonomous driving industry, primarily focused on developing and deploying self-driving technologies. Through its subsidiaries, the Group procures high-definition mapping, data collection, and labeling services to support its autonomous driving business in markets where detailed mapping data is critical.

data.title: Livzon Pharmaceutical’s First-Quarter Profit Slips as Policy Shift and Seasonality Hit Revenue
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest update is out from Livzon Pharmaceutical Group ( (HK:1513) ).

Livzon Pharmaceutical Group reported unaudited first-quarter 2026 results prepared under China Accounting Standards, showing operating income of RMB2.871 billion and net profit attributable to shareholders of RMB577 million. Operating income and net profit declined by 9.73% and 9.39% year on year, respectively, while basic earnings per share fell to RMB0.65 and return on net assets eased to 4.06%.

Management attributed the weaker top line to a transitional period of industry policy adjustments and seasonal fluctuations in influenza and respiratory infections, which weighed on demand in the quarter. Despite the softer revenue and profit, net cash flow from operating activities rose 12.8% to RMB817 million and shareholders’ equity increased 4.47%, indicating stable overall performance and resilient operating quality in the core business.

Total assets edged up 0.87% to RMB24.19 billion by the end of March, underscoring a broadly steady balance sheet position. The board and senior management affirmed the accuracy and completeness of the disclosed financial data, signaling a focus on transparency and compliance for investors amid a challenging policy and market environment.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1513) stock is a Sell with a HK$22.99 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Livzon Pharmaceutical Group stock, see the HK:1513 Stock Forecast page.

More about Livzon Pharmaceutical Group

Livzon Pharmaceutical Group Inc. is a China-based pharmaceutical company listed in Hong Kong, operating as a joint stock company with limited liability. The group focuses on the development, production and sale of pharmaceutical products, serving the domestic healthcare market and leveraging its diversified portfolio to maintain resilient core operations.
